title: Sentiment Hype-Adjustment and News Source Weighting
summary: Implement a news coverage normalisation layer that adjusts raw sentiment scores for over-representation bias. A 2025 PMC paper introduced a Hype-Adjusted Probability Measure, finding that NVDA received 24.52% of semiconductor sector news coverage over 15 months despite being only ~8% of market weight — meaning raw positive NVDA sentiment is inflated by sheer volume. The fix is to weight each ticker's sentiment score by (market_weight / news_coverage_weight). Similarly, weight news sources by credibility tier: major wires > analyst blogs > aggregators. This directly addresses the 'distraction effect' documented by Glasserman & Lin (2023).
expected_impact: Reduces false signals from hyper-covered tickers (e.g. NVDA with 24.5% of semiconductor news but only 8% of market weight); improves signal-to-noise ratio by 15-25% estimated on momentum-heavy watchlists
